notes.hypothesis: After wiping s1 to an air-dry state, placing only s1 into water should create a large rapid raw ADC drop on s1 while the other dry probes remain mostly unchanged.
notes.method: s1 was wiped/air-dried and then placed into the water cup during a 90 s capture at 0.5 s cadence. The other probes remained dry/static. Timing includes manual placement time.
notes.findings: s1 dropped from 3006 to 987 raw ADC, a -2019 count response. s2 stayed effectively unchanged at dry (+0 counts), while s3 drifted +56 and s4 +2 counts.
notes.conclusion: The s1 dry-to-wet response was strong and fast. Across the one-by-one dry-to-wet tests, all four probes showed large water responses and limited immediate cross-channel contamination.
